target.name: unspecified
target.motivation: The paper discusses the development of integrative technologies at the interface between chemistry, biology, informatics, and engineering for sustainable chemical manufacturing, with a focus on biocatalyst development, chemoenzymatic synthesis, and informatics-driven molecular design. It also mentions the discovery and engineering of PET plastic depolymerization enzymes and halogenase work.

success_criteria.free_text: Development of sustainable chemical manufacturing processes, discovery and engineering of enzymes (e.g., PET plastic depolymerization enzymes, halogenases) with improved performance, and integration of automation and AI in biocatalysis.

retrosynthetic_strategy.rationale: The paper focuses on general research philosophy and recent achievements in biocatalysis and enzyme engineering rather than a specific total synthesis of a single target molecule. Therefore, a detailed retrosynthetic strategy is not applicable.

thought.background: Dr. Lim has over 15 years of experience in advancing chemistry frontiers and harnessing nature's catalytic powers. Her research interests include biocatalyst development, chemoenzymatic synthesis, and informatics-driven molecular design.
thought.gap: The need for more sustainable chemical manufacturing processes and accelerated development of biocatalysts.
thought.decision: To address sustainability and speed, the team decided to integrate chemistry, biology, informatics, and engineering, with a focus on biocatalysis and AI-enabled approaches.

outcome.observation: Formulated a research philosophy centered on interdisciplinary expertise and collaborative team work to solve problems in sustainable chemical manufacturing.

evidence.text_span:
Dr Yee Hwee Lim currently leads a team of more than 25 researchers in the Chemical Biotechnology and Biocatalysis division at the A*STAR Institute of Sustainability for Chemicals, Energy and Environment (A*STAR ISCE2 ) to develop integrative technologies at the interface between chemistry, biology, informatics, and engineering for sustainable chemical manufacturing.
Dr Yee Hwee Lim has more than 15 years’ experience advancing chemistry frontiers and harnessing nature’s catalytic powers to solve molecular problems. Her research interests include biocatalyst development, chemoenzymatic synthesis and informatics-driven molecular design.
My research currently focuses on advancing and applying biocatalysis to make molecules more sustainable. Looking ahead, the speed of development of biocatalysts will be accelerated through integration with automation-driven and AI-enabled approaches. In terms of the next breakthrough in biocatalysis, I see harnessing the best of both chemistry and nature’s catalysis as the exciting future to create new-to-nature enzymes to streamline molecular synthesis.
My research philosophy is to harness the sparks of interdisciplinary expertise to solve problems. I strongly believe the power of the sum is greater than its parts and a collaborative team with deep individual expertise can do wonders.
evidence.section: Introduction, What direction do you think your research field should go in?, How would you describe your research philosophy?

thought.background: The team is focused on advancing biocatalysis for sustainable chemical manufacturing.
thought.gap: Existing enzymes for PET plastic depolymerization may not be efficient enough.
thought.decision: To discover and engineer PET plastic depolymerization enzymes using computational/AI methods, expecting to surpass the state of the art.

outcome.observation: Discovered an enzyme mutant through a protein language modelling approach that can surpass the state of the art (unpublished).

evidence.text_span: My team recently delved into the topic of the discovery and engineering of PET plastic depolymerization enzymes. Recently, in collaboration with computational/AI scientists, we discovered an enzyme mutant through a protein language modelling approach that can surpass the state of the art (unpublished).
evidence.section: What scientific development are you currently most excited about?

thought.background: A halogenase enzyme was reported more than 20 years ago but was never applied, likely due to challenges in expressing consistent quality.
thought.gap: The challenge of expressing consistent quality of the halogenase hindered its application.
thought.decision: To solve the long-standing problem of applying this halogenase, expecting to enable its use in synthesis.

outcome.observation: Successfully applied the halogenase enzyme, leading to a publication in 2024. This solved a long-standing problem in the field.
outcome.free_text: The work involved an enzyme that was reported more than 20 years ago but was never applied, likely due to the challenge of expressing consistent quality of the halogenase.

evidence.text_span: For example, the halogenase work that we published in 2024 (https://www.nature.com/ articles/s42004-023-01083-1) involves an enzyme that was reported more than 20 years ago but was never applied. We realized that it was probably due to the challenge of expressing consistent quality of the halogenase that hindered the progress.
evidence.section: What aspects of your research do you find most exciting or most rewarding?

validation.significance: The research focuses on developing sustainable chemical manufacturing processes through biocatalysis and enzyme engineering, integrating AI and automation. Key achievements include the discovery of an improved PET plastic depolymerization enzyme mutant and the successful application of a previously unutilized halogenase enzyme, addressing long-standing challenges in the field.

source.title: Women in chemistry: Q&A with Dr Yee Hwee Lim.
